groups.title: Hinchingbrooke Country Park
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: The park Area: $21632$ hectares Habitats: wetland, grassland and woodland Wetland: lakes, ponds and a $21633$ Wildlife includes birds, insects and animals Subjects studied in educational visits include Science: Children look at $21634$ about plants, etc. Geography: includes learning to use a $21635$ and compass History: changes in land use Leisure and tourism: mostly concentrates on the park’s $21636$ Music: Children make $21637$ with natural materials, and experiment with rhythm and speed. Benefits of outdoor educational visits They give children a feeling of $21638$ that they may not have elsewhere. Children learn new $21639$ and gain self-confidence. Practical issues Cost per child: £ $21640$ Adults, such as $21641$, free

questions.analysis: 题干问的是公园的面积（Area），并且要求单位是公顷（hectares）。在听力原文中，Sally提到“the park covers 170 acres, that's 69 hectares”，明确给出了公园的面积是69公顷。因此，正确答案是“69”或“sixty-nine”。
答案是“stream”，因为听力材料中明确提到“ponds and a stream that flows through the park”。题干中的定位词“Wetland, lakes, ponds”帮助我们在听力材料中快速找到相关信息，并且这些词汇在听力材料中出现时，提示我们答案即将出现。通过抓住这些关键词，考生可以准确地填写答案。
本题的正确答案是“data”，因为听力材料中明确提到“collect and analyse data about the things they see”，与题干中的“look at”相对应。考生需要注意题干中的定位词“Science”、“Children”、“plants”，并在听力过程中关注这些词汇的出现，以便快速定位到答案。
答案是“map”，因为在听力原文中，Sally提到地理活动时说到“learn and practise reading a map and using a compass”，这明确指出了学习和练习使用地图和指南针。因此，题干中要求填写的工具是“map”。
本题的正确答案是“visitors”，因为听力原文明确提到“leisure and tourism”主要集中在“your visitors”。考生需要通过定位词“leisure and tourism”快速找到相关内容，并通过信号词“Then there's leisure and tourism”确认答案位置。理解“concentrates on”的含义是解题的关键。

正确答案是“sounds”，因为听力原文中明确提到“the children experiment with natural materials to create sounds and explore rhythm and tempo”。题干中的定位词“Music”，“natural materials”，“rhythm”，“speed”帮助我们快速定位到相关信息，而信号词“music”，“natural materials”，“rhythm”，“tempo”则提示我们答案即将出现。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过抓住关键词和听取关键句来提高答题准确率。

答案是“freedom”，因为在听力原文中，John提到“they get a sense of freedom that might not be a normal part of their lives”，这与题干中的“feeling of ... that they may not have elsewhere”相对应。题干中的“feeling”在原文中被同义替换为“sense”，而“freedom”则是具体的感受，符合题干要求填入的单词。
这道题的正确答案是“skills”，因为听力材料中明确提到“they develop new skills. This gives them greater self-confidence.”。题干中的“new”和“self-confidence”是定位词，帮助学生在听力材料中快速找到相关信息。通过识别和理解这些关键词，学生可以准确填入正确答案。
答案是“4.95”，因为在听力原文中明确提到“it costs £4.95 for each child who attends on the day”。通过定位词“Cost per child”和信号词“costs”，我们可以准确找到答案所在的句子。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过抓住关键的数字和货币符号来推测正确答案。
答案是“leaders”，因为听力原文明确提到“There's no charge for leaders and other adults”，这与题干中的“Adults, such as 【    】, free”直接对应。通过定位词“Adults”和信号词“free”，学生可以在听力中准确找到答案。

transcript.en: PART 1
SALLY: Good morning. Hinchingbrooke Country Park, Sally speaking. I'm one of the rangers.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：早上好。这里是欣奇布鲁克郊野公园，我是萨莉。我是护林员之一。

transcript.en: JOHN: Oh hello. My name's John Chapman, and I'm a teaching assistant at a local primary school.
transcript.cn: 约翰:哦，你好。我叫约翰·查普曼，是当地一所小学的助教。

transcript.en: I've been asked to arrange a visit to the park for two of our classes.
transcript.cn: 我被要求为我们两个班安排一次公园参观。

transcript.en: SALLY: OK. What would you like to know?
transcript.cn: 萨莉:好的。你想知道些什么?

transcript.en: JOHN: Well, I'm new to this area, so perhaps you could tell me something about the park first, please.
transcript.cn: 约翰:嗯，我对这个地区不熟悉，所以也许你可以先告诉我一些关于公园的情况。

transcript.en: SALLY: Of course. Altogether the park covers 170 acres, that's 69 hectares.
transcript.cn: 萨莉:当然可以。整个公园占地170英亩，也就是69公顷。

transcript.en: There are three main types of habitat: wetland, grassland and woodland.
transcript.cn: 这里主要有三种栖息地:湿地、草地和林地。

transcript.en: The woods are well established and varied, with an oak plantation, and other areas of mixed species.
transcript.cn: 这里的树林生长良好，种类繁多，有一片橡树种植园，还有其他混合树种的区域。

transcript.en: JOHN: Right.
transcript.cn: 约翰:好的。

transcript.en: SALLY: The wetland is quite varied, too.
transcript.cn: 萨莉:这里的湿地也很多样。

transcript.en: The original farmland was dug up around 40 years ago to extract gravel.
transcript.cn: 大约 40 年前，为了开采砾石，人们挖开了原来的农田。

transcript.en: Once this work was completed, the gravel pits filled with water, forming the two large lakes.
transcript.cn: 工程结束后，砾石坑里充满了水，形成了两个大湖。

transcript.en: There are also several smaller ones, ponds and a stream that flows through the park.
transcript.cn: 还有几个较小的湖，池塘和一条流经公园的小溪。

transcript.en: JOHN: OK, so I suppose with these different habitats there's quite a variety of wildlife.
transcript.cn: 约翰：好的，我想这些不同的栖息地里有各种各样的野生动物。

transcript.en: SALLY: There certainly is - a lot of different species of birds and insects, and also animals like deer and rabbits.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：没错，有很多不同种类的鸟类和昆虫，还有鹿和兔子等动物。

transcript.en: JOHN: And I understand you organise educational visits for school parties.
transcript.cn: 约翰：据我所知，你们还为学校组织教育参观活动。

transcript.en: SALLY: That's right. We can organise a wide range of activities and adapt them to suit all ages.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：没错。我们可以组织各种各样的活动，并根据不同年龄段的需要进行调整。

transcript.en: JOHN: Can you give me some examples of the activities?
transcript.cn: 约翰：能举几个活动的例子吗？

transcript.en: SALLY: Well, one focus is on science, where we help children to discover and study plants, trees and insects.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：嗯，其中一个重点是科学，我们帮助孩子们发现和研究植物、树木和昆虫

transcript.en: They also collect and analyse data about the things they see.
transcript.cn: 他们还收集和分析所观察到的相关数据。

transcript.en: JOHN: Uhuh.
transcript.cn: 约翰：嗯。

transcript.en: SALLY: Another focus is on geography.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：另一个重点是地理。

transcript.en: The park is a great environment to learn and practise reading a map and using a compass to navigate around the park.
transcript.cn: 公园是一个学习和练习看地图和使用指南针在公园里导航的绝佳环境。

transcript.en: JOHN: Do you do anything connected with history?
transcript.cn: 约翰：你们做与历史有关的活动吗？

transcript.en: SALLY: Yes, we do. For instance, the children can explore how the use of the land has changed over time. Then there's leisure and tourism.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：是的，我们会做。例如，孩子们可以探索土地的使用随着时间的推移发生了哪些变化。还有休闲和旅游。

transcript.en: JOHN: That focuses on your visitors, I would imagine.
transcript.cn: 约翰：我想，这主要是针对公园的游客的吧。

transcript.en: SALLY: Yes, mostly. The children find out about them, their requirements, the problems they may cause and how we manage these.
transcript.cn: 萨莉:是的，大部分是。孩子们了解他们，他们的要求，他们可能造成的问题，以及我们如何处理这些问题。

transcript.en: And another subject we cover is music: here the children experiment with natural materials to create sounds and explore rhythm and tempo.
transcript.cn: 我们的另一个主题是音乐：在这里，孩子们尝试用天然材料创造声音，探索节奏和节拍。

transcript.en: JOHN: That must be fun!
transcript.cn: 约翰：那一定很有趣！

transcript.en: SALLY: Most children really enjoy it.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：大多数孩子都很喜欢。

transcript.en: SALLY: And of course, all the activities are educational, too.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：当然，所有活动也都具有教育意义。

transcript.en: Learning outside the classroom encourages children to be creative, and to explore and discover for themselves.
transcript.cn: 课外学习鼓励孩子们发挥创造力，自己去探索和发现。

transcript.en: JOHN: I would imagine they get a sense of freedom that might not be a normal part of their lives.
transcript.cn: 约翰:我想他们会有一种自由的感觉，这可能是他们生活中不太常见的事情。

transcript.en: SALLY: That's right. And very often the children discover that they can do things they didn't know they could do, and they develop new skills.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：没错。很多时候，孩子们会发现自己可以做一些他们不知道自己能做的事情，他们会发展出新的技能。

transcript.en: This gives them greater self-confidence.
transcript.cn: 这让他们更加自信。

transcript.en: JOHN: It sounds great. So, what about the practical side of it?
transcript.cn: 约翰：听起来不错。那实际操作起来什么样呢？

transcript.en: How much does it cost for a full-day visit? We would expect to bring between 30 and 40 children.
transcript.cn: 全天参观的费用是多少？我们预计会有 30 到 40 名儿童。

transcript.en: SALLY: If there are over 30, it costs £4.95 for each child who attends on the day.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：如果人数超过 30 人，当天每个孩子的费用为 4.95 英镑。

transcript.en: We invoice you afterwards, so you don't pay for children who can't come because of sickness, for example.
transcript.cn: 我们会在活动结束后为您开具发票，这样你就不用为那些因为生病而不能来的孩子付钱了。

transcript.en: There's no charge for leaders and other adults - as many as you want to bring.
transcript.cn: 领队和其他成年人不收费——你想带多少人就带多少人。

transcript.en: JOHN: That sounds very fair. Well, thanks for all the information.
transcript.cn: 约翰：听起来很不错。谢谢你提供的信息。

transcript.en: I'll need to discuss it with my colleagues, and I hope to get back to you soon to make a booking.
transcript.cn: 我需要和我的同事们讨论一下，希望能尽快给你回复，以便预订。

transcript.en: SALLY: We'll look forward to hearing from you. Goodbye.
transcript.cn: 萨莉：期待你的回电。再见。

transcript.en: JOHN: Goodbye, and thank you.
transcript.cn: 约翰：再见，谢谢你。

groups.title: Stanthorpe Twinning Association
groups.desc:
Questions 11-15
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: During the visit to Malatte, in France, members especially enjoyed
questions.analysis: 正确答案是B. experiencing a river trip。录音中明确提到“theme park trip had to be cancelled”，排除了选项A。而“we all had a great time on the final boat trip down the river – that was the real highlight”则表明了选项B是正确答案。虽然录音中提到参观奶酪工厂，但并没有强调这是他们特别享受的活动，因此排除选项C。

questions.title: What will happen in Stanthorpe to mark the 25th anniversary of the Twinning Association?

options.title: B
options.content: A garden seat will be bought.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是A。听力原文明确提到在Stanthorpe会种植一棵杨树来纪念25周年。学生在听力过程中需要特别注意定位词和信号词，抓住关键动词和名词，才能准确找到答案。

questions.title: Which event raised most funds this year?
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 B. the pancake evening。听力原文中明确提到煎饼晚会出席人数众多，并创下了利润记录，这表明它是筹集资金最多的活动。虽然法国烹饪示范也很成功，但并没有达到煎饼晚会的水平。

questions.title: For the first evening with the French visitors host families are advised to

options.title: A
options.content: take them for a walk round the town.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C. have a meal at home。听力原文中明确提到“have dinner in the house or garden rather than eating out”，这与选项C完全对应。通过定位词和信号词的分析，学生可以快速定位到相关信息，并根据听到的内容做出正确选择。

questions.title: On Saturday evening there will be the chance to
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为听力原文明确提到“we'll have Toby Sharp and his band performing English and Scottish country songs”，这表明周六晚上有音乐表演。通过定位词“Saturday evening”以及信号词“performing”和“songs”，可以准确找到答案。学生需要注意听力中的关键词汇，并结合上下文进行判断。

groups.title: Farley House
groups.desc:
Questions 16-20
Label the map below.
Write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 16-20.

questions.title: Farm shop
questions.analysis: 第16题的正确答案是G，因为听力原文明确提到“farm shop”在“old stables”的左边，而地图上“old stables”位于G位置。通过定位词“farm shop”和信号词“old stables”，可以准确找到答案。

questions.title: Disabled entry
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C，因为听力材料中明确提到无障碍入口在房子远离停车场的一侧。根据地图，选项C位于房子的远侧，且远离停车场，符合听力材料中的描述。

questions.title: Adventure playground
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文，冒险游乐场位于较大湖的北端。地图上标记为B的位置正好符合这个描述，因为它位于较大湖的北端。因此，正确答案是B。

questions.title: Kitchen gardens
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文，Temple of the Four Winds 的位置描述如下：从停车场出发，沿着路径走，经过马厩和房子的西侧。当路径分叉时，走右边的路径。左边是树林，继续走到尽头。根据地图，选项 D 符合这些描述，因此选项 D 是正确答案。

questions.title: The Temple of the Four Winds
questions.analysis: 第20题的正确答案是A，因为听力原文详细描述了从停车场出发，经过马厩和房子的西侧，在路径分叉处选择右侧路径，左侧是树林，最终到达终点的位置。根据这些描述，A是唯一符合这些路径描述的位置。

transcript.en: PART 2
It's great to see so many members of the Twinning Association here tonight.
transcript.cn: 很高兴今晚在这里看到这么多结对协会的成员。

transcript.en: Since the twinning link between our two towns, Stanthorpe here in England and Malatte in France, was established, the relationship between the towns has gone from strength to strength.
transcript.cn: 自从英国的斯坦索普镇和法国的马拉特镇结为友好城市以来，两镇之间的关系日益密切。

transcript.en: Last month, 25 members of the association from Stanthorpe spent a weekend in Malatte.
transcript.cn: 上个月，来自斯坦索普的 25 名协会成员在马拉特度过了一个周末。

transcript.en: Our hosts had arranged a great programme.
transcript.cn: 东道主为我们安排了精彩的节目。

transcript.en: We learned how cheese is produced in the region and had the chance to taste the products.
transcript.cn: 我们了解了该地区奶酪的生产过程，并有机会品尝了奶酪产品。

transcript.en: The theme park trip had to be cancelled, but we all had a great time on the final boat trip down the river - that was the real highlight.
transcript.cn: 主题公园之旅不得不取消，但我们都在最后的乘船游河之旅中度过了美好时光--这才是真正的亮点。

transcript.en: This is a special year for the Association because it's 25 years since we were founded.
transcript.cn: 今年是协会成立25周年的特殊年份。

transcript.en: In Malatte, they're planning to mark this by building a footbridge in the municipal park.
transcript.cn: 在马拉特，他们计划在市政公园修建一座天桥来纪念这个日子。

transcript.en: We've been discussing what to do here and we've decided to plant a poplar tree in the museum gardens.
transcript.cn: 我们一直在讨论该在这里做些什么，最后决定在博物馆花园里种植一棵白杨树。

transcript.en: We considered buying a garden seat to put there, but the authorities weren't happy with that idea.
transcript.cn: 我们曾考虑买一个花园座椅放在那里，但当局对这一想法并不满意。

transcript.en: In terms of fundraising to support our activities, we've done very well. Our pancake evening was well attended and made record profits.
transcript.cn: 在筹款支持我们的活动方面，我们做得很好。我们的煎饼之夜出席人数很多，利润也创了记录。

transcript.en: And everyone enjoyed the demonstration of French cookery, which was nearly as successful.
transcript.cn: 大家都很喜欢法式烹饪的展示，同样也很成功。

transcript.en: Numbers for our film show were limited because of the venue so we're looking for somewhere bigger next year.
transcript.cn: 由于场地的原因，我们的电影放映人数有限，所以我们明年要找一个更大的地方。

transcript.en: We're looking forward to welcoming our French visitors here next week, and I know that many of you here will be hosting individuals or families.
transcript.cn: 我们期待着下周在这里迎接我们的法国客人，我知道在座的许多人都将接待个人或家庭。

transcript.en: The coach from France will arrive at 5 pm on Friday. Don't try to do too much that first evening as they'll be tired, so have dinner in the house or garden rather than eating out.
transcript.cn: 周五下午 5 点，来自法国的旅游巴士将抵达。第一天晚上不要做太多事情，因为他们会很累，所以晚餐可以在家里或花园里吃，不要在外面吃。

transcript.en: The weather looks as if it'll be OK so you might like to plan a barbecue.
transcript.cn: 天气看起来会很不错，所以你们可以计划一次烧烤。

transcript.en: Then the next morning's market day in town, and that's always a good place to stroll round.
transcript.cn: 第二天早晨，镇上有集市，那会是一个闲逛的好地方。

transcript.en: On Saturday evening, we'll all meet up at the football club, where once again we'll have Toby Sharp and his band performing English and Scottish country songs.
transcript.cn: 周六晚上，我们将在足球俱乐部集合，托比-夏普（Toby Sharp）和他的乐队将再次为我们演唱英格兰和苏格兰乡村歌曲。

transcript.en: Toby will already be well known to many of you as last year he organised our special quiz night and presented the prizes.
transcript.cn: 你们很多人都知道Toby，因为去年他组织了一场特别的智力竞赛之夜并颁发了奖品。

transcript.en: Now on Sunday, we'll be taking our visitors to Farley House. You may not all be familiar with it, so here's a map to help you.
transcript.cn: 周日，我们将带客人去法利故居。有人可能不太熟悉这里，所以这里有一张地图可以帮助大家。

transcript.en: You can see the car park at the bottom of the map.
transcript.cn: 你可以在地图的底部看到停车场。

transcript.en: There's an excellent farm shop in the grounds where our visitors can buy local produce - it's in the old stables, which is the first building you come to.
transcript.cn: 院子里有一家很不错的农家商店，游客可以在那里买到当地特产——它在老马厩里，这是你来到的第一个建筑。

transcript.en: They're built round a courtyard, and the shop's in the far corner on the left. There's also a small café on the right as you go in.
transcript.cn: 它们围绕一个庭院而建，商店在最左边的角落里。进门右侧还有一家小咖啡馆。

transcript.en: I know that one or two of our visitors may not be all that mobile.
transcript.cn: 我知道有一两个访客可能行动不便。

transcript.en: The main entrance to the house has a lot of steps so you might want to use the disabled entry. This is on the far side of the house from the car park.
transcript.cn: 房屋的正门有很多台阶，因此您可能需要使用残疾人入口。这个入口位于房屋离停车场较远的那一侧。

transcript.en: Children will probably be most interested in the adventure playground.
transcript.cn: 孩子们可能会对冒险游乐场最感兴趣。

transcript.en: That's at the northern end of the larger lake, in a bend on the path that leads to the lake. There's lots for children to do there.
transcript.cn: 那是在大湖的北端，在通向湖的小路上的一个拐弯处。那里有很多适合孩子们玩耍的地方。

transcript.en: There are a number of lovely gardens near the house.
transcript.cn: 房子附近有许多漂亮的花园。

transcript.en: The kitchen gardens are rectangular and surrounded by a wall.
transcript.cn: 厨房花园呈长方形，四周有围墙环绕。

transcript.en: They're to the north-east of the house, quite near the smaller lake. They're still in use and have a great collection of fruit and vegetables.
transcript.cn: 它们在房子的东北方向，离小湖很近。它们仍在使用，并收集了大量水果和蔬菜。

transcript.en: The Temple of the Four Winds is a bit more of a walk - but it's worth it.
transcript.cn: 去四风寺需要走一段路，但值得一去。

transcript.en: Take the path from the car park and go past the western sides of the stables and the house.
transcript.cn: 从停车场沿着小路走，经过马厩和房屋的西侧。

transcript.en: Then when the path forks, take the right-hand path. Go up there with the woods on your left and the temple is right at the end.
transcript.cn: 然后在岔路口向右拐，继续往前走，左边是树林，寺庙就在右边的尽头。

transcript.en: There are great views over the whole area.
transcript.cn: 从这里可以俯瞰整片区域的美丽景色。

transcript.en: OK, so that's…
transcript.cn: 好的，那么…

groups.title: Which TWO things did Colin find most satisfying about his bread reuse project?
groups.desc:
Questions 21-22
Choose TWO letters, A–E
Write your answers in boxes 21-22 on your answer sheet.

list.title: B
list.content: finding a good way to prevent waste

list.title: C
list.content: overcoming problems in a basic process

collect_option.title: Which TWO things did Colin find most satisfying about his bread reuse project?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B和D。科林在项目中最满意的两点分别是：找到了一种防止浪费的好方法（B），以及在设计和颜色上进行实验（D）。
这道题的正确答案是B和D。科林在项目中最满意的两点分别是：找到了一种防止浪费的好方法（B），以及在设计和颜色上进行实验（D）。

groups.title: Which TWO ways do the students agree that touch-sensitive sensors for food labels could be developed in future?
groups.desc:
Questions 23-24
Choose two letters from the A-E
Write your answers in boxes 23-24 on your answer sheet.

list.title: B
list.content: to show that food is no longer fit to eat

list.title: C
list.content: for use with drinks as well as foods

list.title: D
list.content: to provide applications for blind people

list.title: E
list.content: to indicate the weight of certain foods

collect_option.title: Which TWO ways do the students agree that touch-sensitive sensors for food labels could be developed in future?

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 A 和 E。通过定位词和信号词的分析，可以准确找到听力材料中对应的句子和关键词。对于基础较差的学生，建议使用关键词法和排除法，并在听力过程中快速记下关键词，帮助确认答案。易错点主要在于混淆现有功能和未来发展的新用途，考生需要特别注意听力材料中对未来发展的讨论。
本题的正确答案是 A 和 E。通过定位词和信号词的分析，可以准确找到听力材料中对应的句子和关键词。对于基础较差的学生，建议使用关键词法和排除法，并在听力过程中快速记下关键词，帮助确认答案。易错点主要在于混淆现有功能和未来发展的新用途，考生需要特别注意听力材料中对未来发展的讨论。

groups.title: Opinions
groups.desc:
Questions 25-30
What is the students’ opinion about each of the following food trends?
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 25–30.

list.title: A
list.content: This is only relevant to young people.

list.title: C
list.content: This already seems to be widespread.

list.title: D
list.content: Retailers should do more to encourage this.

list.title: E
list.content: More financial support is needed for this.

list.title: F
list.content: Most people know little about this.

list.title: G
list.content: There should be stricter regulations about this.

questions.title: Use of local products
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是D. Retailers should do more to encourage this。对话中提到“they need to be more proactive to address that”，意思是他们需要更加积极地解决这个问题，这表明零售商应该做更多的工作来鼓励使用本地产品。

questions.title: Reduction in unnecessary packaging
questions.analysis: 正确答案是G，因为听力原文明确提到“政府需要采取行动”，这与选项G中的“应该有更严格的法规”相符。学生在听力过程中需要特别注意与政府和法规相关的词汇，以便正确理解题意并选择正确答案。

questions.title: Gluten-free and lactose-free food
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C. This already seems to be widespread. 这是因为听力原文中提到“lots of people I know have been buying that type of food for years now”，表明这种食物已经被广泛购买和使用。通过定位词“Gluten-free and lactose-free food”以及信号词“allergies or food intolerances”，可以快速定位到相关信息，并通过关键词“lots of people”和“for years now”确认答案。

questions.title: Use of branded products related to celebrity chefs
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是B. This may have disappointing results. 这是因为对话中Marie和Colin都提到他们购买的名厨品牌产品并不令人满意，Marie说她不会再买，Colin说产品没有味道。这些负面评价表明他们对这种趋势感到失望。

questions.title: Development of ‘ghost kitchens’ for takeaway food
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是F. Most people know little about this. 这是因为听力材料中明确提到“people aren't aware of that”，即大多数人对“ghost kitchens”了解不多。通过抓住关键词“ghost kitchens”和信号词“people aren't aware of that”，可以准确定位到答案。

questions.title: Use of mushrooms for common health concerns
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 H. This could be dangerous. 因为听力原文中明确提到使用蘑菇治疗健康问题可能存在很大的风险（“taking a big risk”），并且难以确定哪些品种是安全的（“hard to know which varieties are safe to eat”）。通过抓住关键词和信号词，学生可以更准确地定位到相关信息，并理解说话者的观点。

transcript.en: PART 3
COLIN: I haven't seen you for a bit, Marie.
transcript.cn: 柯林：我有段时间没见你了，玛丽。

transcript.en: MARIE: No. I've been busy with my project.
transcript.cn: 玛丽：是的，我一直在忙我的项目。

transcript.en: COLIN: You're making a vegan alternative to eggs, aren't you? Something that doesn't use animal products?
transcript.cn: 柯林:你在做鸡蛋的素食替代品，是吗?不使用动物制品的东西?

transcript.en: MARIE: Yes. I'm using chickpeas. I had two main aims when I first started looking for an alternative to eggs, but actually I've found chickpeas have got more advantages.
transcript.cn: 玛丽:是的。我用的是鹰嘴豆。我刚开始寻找鸡蛋的替代品时有两个主要目的，但实际上我发现鹰嘴豆有更多的优点。

transcript.en: COLIN: Right.
transcript.cn: 柯林：没错。

transcript.en: MARIE: But how about your project on reusing waste food - you were looking at bread, weren't you?
transcript.cn: 玛丽：那你关于废弃食物再利用的项目如何--你在研究面包，是吗？

transcript.en: COLIN: Yes. It's been hard work, but I've enjoyed it.
transcript.cn: 柯林：是的。 工作很辛苦，但我乐在其中。

transcript.en: The basic process was quite straightforward - breaking the stale bread down to a paste then reforming it.
transcript.cn: 基本过程非常简单--把过期面包打成糊状，然后重新加工。

transcript.en: MARIE: But you were using 3-D printing, weren't you, to make the paste into biscuits?
transcript.cn: 玛丽：但你是用3d打印把浆糊做成饼干的，对吗?

transcript.en: COLIN: Yeah, I'd used that before, but in this project, I had time to play around with different patterns for the biscuits and finding how I could add fruit and vegetables to make them a more appetising colour, and I was really pleased with what I managed to produce.
transcript.cn: 柯林:是的，我以前用过，但在这个项目中，我有时间为饼干尝试不同的图案，并找到如何添加水果和蔬菜，使它们的颜色更有食欲，我真的很满意我成功做出来的东西。

transcript.en: MARIE: It must've been a great feeling to make something appetising out of bits of old bread that would've been thrown away otherwise.
transcript.cn: 玛丽：能用那些本来会被扔掉的旧面包做成美味佳肴，那种感觉一定很棒。

transcript.en: COLIN: It was. And I'm hoping that some of the restaurants in town will be interested in the biscuits. I'm going to send them some samples.
transcript.cn: 柯林：是的。我希望镇上的一些餐馆会对这种饼干感兴趣。我要给他们寄一些样品。

transcript.en: MARIE: I came across something on the internet yesterday that might interest you. It was a company that's developed touch-sensitive sensors for food labels.
transcript.cn: 玛丽：我昨天在网上看到了一些东西，你可能会感兴趣。那是一家为食品标签开发触摸感应器的公司。

transcript.en: COLIN: Mmm?
transcript.cn: 柯林：嗯？

transcript.en: MARIE: It's a special sort of label on the food package. When the label's smooth, the food is fresh and then when you can feel bumps on the label that means the food's gone bad.
transcript.cn: 玛丽：这是食品包装上的一种特殊标签。当标签光滑时，说明食物新鲜；当你能感觉到标签上有凸起，说明食物已经变质了。

transcript.en: It started off as a project to help visually impaired people know whether food was fit to eat or not.
transcript.cn: 它最初是一个帮助视障人士了解食物是否适合食用的项目。

transcript.en: COLIN: Interesting. So just solid food?
transcript.cn: 柯林:有意思。所以只用于固体食物吗?

transcript.en: MARIE: No, things like milk and juice as well. But actually, I thought it might be really good for drug storage in hospitals and pharmacies.
transcript.cn: 玛丽:不，还有牛奶和果汁之类的东西。但实际上，我认为它可能非常适合医院和药房的药品储存。

transcript.en: COLIN: Right. And coming back to food, maybe it'd be possible to use it for other things besides freshness. Like how many kilograms a joint of meat is, for example.
transcript.cn: 柯林:没错。说回食品，也许除了新鲜度，它还可以用于其他方面。比如一块肉有多少公斤。

transcript.en: MARIE: Yes, there's all sorts of possibilities.
transcript.cn: 玛丽:是的，有各种各样的可能性。

transcript.en: COLIN: I was reading an article about food trends predicting how eating habits might change in the next few years.
transcript.cn: 柯林：我读过一篇关于食品趋势的文章，预测了未来几年饮食习惯可能发生的变化。

transcript.en: MARIE: Oh - things like more focus on local products? That seems so obvious, but the shops are still full of imported foods.
transcript.cn: 玛丽：哦，比如更多地关注本地产品？这似乎是很显而易见的，但商店里仍然摆满了进口食品。

transcript.en: COLIN: Yes, they need to be more proactive to address that.
transcript.cn: 柯林：是的，他们需要更加积极主动地解决这个问题。

transcript.en: MARIE: And somehow motivate consumers to change, yes.
transcript.cn: 玛丽：对，并且以某种方式促使消费者改变。

transcript.en: COLIN: One thing everyone's aware of is the need for a reduction in unnecessary packaging - but just about everything you buy in supermarkets is still covered in plastic. The government needs to do something about it.
transcript.cn: 柯林：大家都意识到需要减少不必要的包装，但在超市里买到的几乎所有东西都还是塑料包装。政府应该为此做点什么。

transcript.en: MARIE: Absolutely. It's got to change.
transcript.cn: 玛丽:当然。这种情况必须改变。

transcript.en: COLIN: Do you think there'll be more interest in gluten- and lactose-free food?
transcript.cn: 柯林：你认为人们会对无麸质和无乳糖食品更感兴趣吗？

transcript.en: MARIE: For people with allergies or food intolerances? I don't know.
transcript.cn: 玛丽:针对过敏或食物不耐受的人?我不是很清楚。

transcript.en: Lots of people I know have been buying that type of food for years now.
transcript.cn: 我知道很多人多年来一直购买这种类型的食品。

transcript.en: COLIN: Yes, even if they haven't been diagnosed with an allergy.
transcript.cn: 柯林:是的，即使他们没有被诊断出过敏。

transcript.en: MARIE: That's right. One thing I've noticed is the number of branded products related to celebrity chefs - people watch them cooking on TV and then buy things like spice mixes or frozen foods with the chef's name on . . . I bought something like that  once, but I won't again.
transcript.cn: 玛丽：没错。我注意到的一件事是，与明星厨师有关的品牌产品越来越多--人们在电视上看他们做菜，然后购买带有名厨名字的调味品或冷冻食品…… 我买过一次这样的东西，但不会再买了。

transcript.en: COLIN: Yeah - I bought a ready-made spice mix for chicken which was supposed to be used by a chef I'd seen on television, and it didn't actually taste of anything.
transcript.cn: 柯林:是的，我买了一个现成的鸡肉调味料，应该是我在电视上看到的一个厨师用的，但实际上它没有任何味道。

transcript.en: MARIE: Mm. Did the article mention 'ghost kitchens' used to produce takeaway food?
transcript.cn: 玛丽:嗯，那篇文章有没有提到用来生产外卖食品的“幽灵厨房”

transcript.en: COLIN: No. What are they?
transcript.cn: 柯林:没有。那是什么?

transcript.en: MARIE: Well, they might have the name of a restaurant, but actually they're a cooking facility just for delivery meals - the public don't ever go there.
transcript.cn: 玛丽：嗯，他们可能挂一个餐馆的名字，但实际上是一个只提供外卖的烹饪场所——大众从来不会去那里。

transcript.en: But people aren't aware of that - it's all kept very quiet.
transcript.cn: 但人们并没有意识到这一点——这一切都被保密了。

transcript.en: COLIN: So people don't realise the food's not actually from the restaurant?
transcript.cn: 柯林:所以人们没有意识到这些食物实际上不是来自餐馆吗?

transcript.en: MARIE: Right.
transcript.cn: 玛丽:没错。

transcript.en: COLIN: Did you know more and more people are using all sorts of different mushrooms now, to treat different health concerns? Things like heart problems?
transcript.cn: 柯林:你知道吗，现在越来越多的人用各种各样的蘑菇来治疗不同的健康问题，诸如心脏病?

transcript.en: MARIE: Hmm. They might be taking a big risk there.
transcript.cn: 玛丽:嗯。他们可能会承担很大的风险。

transcript.en: COLIN: Yes, it's hard to know which varieties are safe to eat.
transcript.cn: 柯林:是的，很难知道哪些品种可以安全食用。

transcript.en: Anyway maybe now . . .
transcript.cn: 不管怎样，也许现在……

groups.title: Céide Fields
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: • an important Neolithic archaeological site in the northwest of Ireland Discovery • In the 1930s, a local teacher realised that stones beneath the bog surface were once $21662$. • His $21663$ became an archaeologist and undertook an investigation of the site;     - a traditional method used by local people to dig for $21664$ was used to identify where stones were located     - carbon dating later proved the site was Neolithic. • Items are well preserved in the bog because of a lack of $21665$. Neolithic farmers • Houses were $21666$ in shape and had a hole in the roof. • Neolithic innovations include:     - cooking indoors     - pots used for storage and to make $21667$. • Each field at Céide was large enough to support a big $21668$. • The fields were probably used to restrict the grazing of animals – no evidence of structures to house them during $21669$. Reasons for the decline in farming • a decline in $21670$ quality • an increase in $21671$

questions.analysis: 答案是“walls”，因为听力原文中明确提到“he realised that these must be walls”。通过定位词“1930s, local teacher, stones, bog surface”以及信号词“1930s, local teacher, stones, bog”，可以快速定位到相关句子，并找到正确答案。
题干中的“His”指代的是Patrick Caulfield，而原文中提到“Patrick Caulfield's son Seamus, who had become an archaeologist by then, began to explore further”，说明他的儿子Seamus成为了考古学家并进行了调查。因此，正确答案是“son”。

答案是“fuel”，因为听力原文明确提到“a traditional method which local people had always used for finding fuel buried in the bog for thousands of years”。通过定位词“traditional method”和“local people”，以及信号词“used for finding”，可以准确找到答案所在的句子，并确定答案是“fuel”。

本题的正确答案是“oxygen”，因为听力材料明确提到“Objects remain so well preserved in these conditions because of the acidity of the peat and the deficiency of oxygen”。考生需要通过定位词“well preserved”、“bog”、“lack of”快速定位到相关信息，并通过信号词“deficiency”确认答案。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过抓住关键词和信号词来猜测正确答案。
答案是“rectangular”，因为听力原文明确提到房屋的形状是矩形的（rectangular），并且屋顶上有一个小洞（small hole in the roof）。题干中的“shape”和“hole in the roof”分别对应了原文中的“rectangular”和“small hole in the roof”。通过这些关键词的对应关系，可以准确找到答案。
正确答案是“lamps”，因为听力原文中明确提到罐子（pots）除了用于储存食物（storing food）外，还可以装脂肪并点燃，作为灯（lamps）使用。题干要求填入的是罐子的另一种用途，因此答案是“lamps”。
答案是 "family"。题干中的定位词 "large enough to support" 在原文中被同义替换为 "sustain"。原文提到 "each plot of land was of a suitable size to sustain an extended family"，这表明每块田地的大小足以支持一个大家庭。因此，正确答案是 "family"。

正确答案是“winter”。题干中的定位词是“no evidence of structures”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“no structures have been found”。听力原文提到“no structures have been found which would have been used to shelter animals in the winter”，这表明在冬季没有发现用于庇护动物的结构，因此答案是“winter”。
答案是“soil”，因为听力原文中明确提到“the soil would have become less productive”，这与题干中的“a decline in”相对应。通过定位词“decline”和信号词“less productive”，可以准确找到答案。

正确答案是“rain”，因为听力原文中明确提到“the conditions became wetter as there was a lot more rain”，即“雨水增加导致条件变得更湿润”。题干中的“increase”是定位词，听力中的“a lot more”是信号词，帮助我们定位到答案“rain”。学生在听力过程中应特别注意这些关键词和信号词，即使基础较差，也能通过抓住这些关键点来找到正确答案。

transcript.en: PART 4
For my presentation today, I'm going to talk about the Céide Fields in the northwest of Ireland, one of the largest Neolithic sites in the world.
transcript.cn: 在今天的演讲中，我将介绍爱尔兰西北部的塞伊德田野，这是世界上最大的新石器时代遗址之一。

transcript.en: I recently visited this site and observed the work that is currently being done by a team of archaeologists there.
transcript.cn: 我最近参观了这个遗址，并观察了考古队目前正在那里进行的工作。

transcript.en: The site was first discovered in the 1930s by a local teacher, Patrick Caulfield.20
transcript.cn: 世纪30年代，当地教师帕特里克·考尔菲尔德(Patrick Caulfield)首次发现了这个遗址。

transcript.en: He noticed that when local people were digging in the bog, they were constantly hitting against what seemed to be rows of stones.
transcript.cn: 他注意到，当地人在沼泽地里挖掘时，会不断撞击到一排排石头。

transcript.en: He realised that these must be walls and that they must be thousands of years old for them to predate the bog which subsequently grew over them.
transcript.cn: 他意识到这些石头一定是城墙，而且有数千年的历史，在沼泽覆盖这片土地前就已经存在了。

transcript.en: He wrote to the National Museum in Dublin to ask them to investigate, but no one took him seriously.
transcript.cn: 他写信给都柏林的国家博物馆，要求他们进行调查，但没有人把他的话当回事。

transcript.en: It wasn't until 40 years later, when Patrick Caulfield's son Seamus, who had become an archaeologist by then, began to explore further.
transcript.cn: 直到40年后，帕特里克·考尔菲尔德的儿子谢默斯(Seamus)才开始进一步探索，当时他已经成为一名考古学家。

transcript.en: He inserted iron probes into the bog to map the formation of the stones, a traditional method which local people had always used for finding fuel buried in the bog for thousands of years.
transcript.cn: 他在沼泽中插入铁探针，用以绘制石头的形成图，这是当地人几千年来一直使用的传统方法，以寻找埋在沼泽中的燃料。

transcript.en: Carbon dating later proved that the site was over 5,000 years old and was the largest Neolithic site in Ireland.
transcript.cn: 碳年代测定法后来证明，该遗址距今已有五千多年的历史，是爱尔兰最大的新石器时代遗址。

transcript.en: Thanks to the bog which covers the area, the remains of the settlement at Céide Fields, which is over 5,000 years old, are extremely well-preserved.
transcript.cn: 由于该遗址被沼泽覆盖，赛德菲尔德定居点遗址保存的非常好，这些遗址有五千多年的历史了。

transcript.en: A bog is 90 percent water; its soil is so saturated that when the grasses and heathers that grow on its surface die, they don't fully decay but accumulate in layers.
transcript.cn: 沼泽 90% 都是水；它的土壤水份非常饱和，以至于生长在其表面的草和红叶石楠死后不会完全腐烂，而是层层堆积。

transcript.en: Objects remain so well preserved in these conditions because of the acidity of the peat and the deficiency of oxygen.
transcript.cn: 由于泥炭的酸性和缺氧，物品在这种条件下依然保存得很好。

transcript.en: At least 175 days of rain a year are required for this to happen; this part of Ireland gets an average of 225 days.
transcript.cn: 要达到这样的条件，每年至少要有175天的降雨；而爱尔兰这一地区的平均降雨天数为 225 天。

transcript.en: The Neolithic farmers at Céide would have enjoyed several centuries of relative peace and stability.
transcript.cn: 在新石器时代，赛伊德地区的农民享受了几个世纪的和平稳定。

transcript.en: Neolithic farmers generally lived in larger communities than their predecessors, with a number of houses built around a community building.
transcript.cn: 与前人相比，新石器时代的农民一般居住在更大的社区中，在社区建筑周围建造了许多房子。

transcript.en: As they lived in permanent settlements, Neolithic farmers were able to build bigger houses.
transcript.cn: 他们住在永久性定居点,新石器时代的农民能够建立更大的房子。

transcript.en: These weren't round as people often assume, but rectangular with a small hole in the roof that allowed smoke to escape.
transcript.cn: 这些房子并不像人们通常认为的那样是圆形的，而是长方形的，屋顶上有一个小孔，可以让烟雾排出。

transcript.en: This is one of many innovations and indicates that the Neolithic farmers were the first people to cook indoors.
transcript.cn: 这是众多创新之一，表明新石器时代的农民是最早在室内做饭的人。

transcript.en: Another new technology that Neolithic settlers brought to Ireland was pottery. Fragments of Neolithic pots have been found in Céide and elsewhere in Ireland.
transcript.cn: 新石器时代移民带到爱尔兰的另一项新技术是制陶。在塞伊德和爱尔兰其他地方都发现了新石器时代的陶器碎片。

transcript.en: The pots were used for many things; as well as for storing food, pots were filled with a small amount of fat and when this was set alight, they served as lamps.
transcript.cn: 罐子的用途很多。除了用来储存食物，罐子里还装有少量脂肪，点燃后就成了灯。

transcript.en: It's thought that the Céide Fields were mainly used as paddocks for animals to graze in.
transcript.cn: 人们认为，塞伊德田野主要被用作牲畜吃草的围场。

transcript.en: Evidence from the Céide Fields suggests that each plot of land was of a suitable size to sustain an extended family.
transcript.cn: 塞伊德田野的证据表明，每块土地的面积都适合维持一个大家庭的生活。

transcript.en: They may have used a system of rotational grazing in order to prevent over-grazing and to allow for plant recovery and regrowth.
transcript.cn: 为防止过度放牧，他们采用了轮牧制度以防止过度放牧，使得植物得以恢复和重新生长。

transcript.en: This must have been a year-round activity as no structures have been found which would have been used to shelter animals in the winter.
transcript.cn: 这必定是一项全年都在进行的活动，因为目前还没有发现在冬季用于动物栖息的建筑。

transcript.en: However, archaeologists believe that this way of life at Céide ceased abruptly. Why was this?
transcript.cn: 然而，考古学家认为，塞伊德的这种生活方式突然停止了。这是为什么呢？

transcript.en: Well, several factors may have contributed to the changing circumstances.
transcript.cn: 有几个因素可能导致了环境的变化。

transcript.en: The soil would have become less productive and led to the abandonment of farming.
transcript.cn: 土壤的生产力会降低，导致人们放弃耕作。

transcript.en: The crop rotation system was partly responsible for this as it would have been very intensive and was not sustainable.
transcript.cn: 轮种制度是造成这种情况的部分原因，因为轮种会非常密集，无法持续。

transcript.en: But there were also climatic pressures too.
transcript.cn: 不过，气候也是原因之一。

transcript.en: The farmers at Céide would have enjoyed a relatively dry period, but this began to change and the conditions became wetter as there was a lot more rain.
transcript.cn: 塞伊德的农民本来可以享受一段相对干燥的时期，但情况开始发生变化，随着雨水增多，环境变得更加潮湿。

transcript.en: It was these conditions that encouraged the bog to form over the area which survives today.
transcript.cn: 正是这些条件促使沼泽在这一地区形成，并延续至今。

transcript.en: So now I'd like to show you some…
transcript.cn: 现在我想给你们看一些……
